sb_prompt: FIND OUT WHAT KIND OF PETS THE OTHER CALLER HAS.
raw_output:
ParticipantA: How are you doing today?
ParticipantB: I'm doing well, thank you. How about you?
ParticipantA: I'm doing pretty good too. So, what kind of pets do you have?
ParticipantB: I have a dog named Biscuit. He's a beagle mix and he's the sweetest thing ever.
ParticipantA: Oh, I love beagles! I have a beagle too! What's your beagle's name?
ParticipantB: Her name is Bailey. She's almost 3 years old and is the light of my life.
ParticipantA: That's so great! My Bailey is also almost 3 years old. She's such a good dog. Do you have any other pets?
ParticipantB: I also have a cat named Whiskers. He's a tuxedo cat and he's so playful.
ParticipantA: Oh, I used to have a tuxedo cat when I was growing up. He was such a cute cat. Do you have any other pets?
ParticipantB: No, just Biscuit and Whiskers. I'm pretty content with just those two.
ParticipantA: I understand. I have Bailey and a fish named Flipper. My fish is a pretty low maintenance pet, but I love having him around.
ParticipantB: Yeah, I hear you. Biscuit and Whiskers are my world, but they can also be a handful sometimes.
ParticipantA: For sure. Pets are a lot of work, but they're also so worth it. Have you ever considered getting another dog?
ParticipantB: I have thought about it, but I don't know if I want to go through the hassle of training another dog. Plus, Biscuit is pretty spoiled and I'm not sure he'd be too happy about it.
ParticipantA: I hear you. Training a new dog can be a lot of work, but it's also a great way to strengthen your bond with your pet. My Bailey is very well-behaved and I know it's because we put in the time and effort to train her.
ParticipantB: That's great to hear. Maybe I'll consider it in the future, but for now, Biscuit and Whiskers are enough for me.
ParticipantA: No problem. They're definitely more than enough work on their own. So, what kind of activities do you like to do with your pets?
ParticipantB: I love going on walks with Biscuit and playing fetch with him. I also like to cuddle with Whiskers on the couch.
ParticipantA: That sounds great. I love taking Bailey on long walks too. She's such a good hiker. And I like to play fetch with her and tug-of-war.
ParticipantB: Oh, that sounds like so much fun! Bailey sounds like a very active dog.
ParticipantA: Yes, she loves to play and be active. She's definitely my workout partner. Do you have any other hobbies besides spending time with your pets?
ParticipantB: I enjoy reading and watching TV. I also like to spend time with my family and friends.
ParticipantA: That's great. I enjoy reading too and also like to spend time with my family and friends. What kind of books do you like to read?
ParticipantB: I like to read mystery novels and true crime stories. I find them very interesting.
ParticipantA: Oh, I love true crime stories too! What's your favorite true crime story?
ParticipantB: My favorite true crime story is "The Stranger Beside Me" by Ann Rule. It's about a serial killer who worked as a nurse and
